text: We arrived as two people carrying the weight of separate lives. We left as one breath. The forest held us in ways words cannot.
Time dissolved. We watched fish through the glass floor at 3am, held hands, and said nothing. It was everything.
At that altitude, pretense falls away. We cried, we laughed, we sat in the hot spring until stars appeared. Transformative.
The paradox of finding silence in Tokyo. The penthouse became our world. We cooked together, danced to vinyl, forgot the city existed.
Walking barefoot on rope bridges, we remembered why we chose each other. The jungle stripped away everything unnecessary.
Every sunset felt like it was painted just for us. The volcanic stone holds warmth long after the sun sets, just like this place holds you.
Floating above clouds in a glass pod. We whispered things we'd never said at sea level. The altitude gave us courage.
Barcelona hummed below us while we existed in our own frequency. The rooftop garden at midnight was our cathedral.
